name: Historic loop between Pau, Tarbes and Lourdes
name_original: Boucle historique entre Pau, Tarbes et Lourdes
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Bordes are set within the Pyrénées-Atlantiques department, offering diverse terrain from valley routes to mountain passes. The region is characterized by its proximity to the Pyrenees mountain range, featuring varied landscapes suitable for different cycling preferences. This area provides a mix of challenging climbs and scenic journeys through lush valleys. Best touring cycling routes around Bordes The most popular touring cycling route is Historic loop between Pau, Tarbes and Lourdes, a 42.8 miles (68.8 km) trail that takes 4 hours 13 minutes to complete. This route connects significant towns, offering a blend of cultural landmarks and varied terrain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Pont de Nay – Col du Soulor loop from Assat, a difficult 57.6 miles (92.7 km) path. This route features substantial elevation gain, leading through mountainous landscapes and offering panoramic views. Local touring cyclists also love the Baudreix Footbridge – View of the Nay Bridge loop from Baliros, a 13.3 miles (21.4 km) trail leading through river valleys, often completed in about 1 hour 18 minutes. Touring cycling around Bordes is defined by mountain passes, river-carved valleys, and dramatic landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate gradients to challenging climbs. The routes in Bordes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.37 stars from more than 300 reviews. More than 4000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Bordes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Bordes?
A: There are over 230 touring cycling routes in the Bordes area, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.37 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes near Bordes?
A: The terrain around Bordes is diverse, ranging from challenging mountain passes like the &lt;strong&gt;Col d'Aubisque&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;Col du Tourmalet&lt;/strong&gt; for experienced cyclists, to more moderate gradients through scenic river-carved valleys such as the &lt;strong&gt;Vallée d'Ossau&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;Vallée d'Aspe&lt;/strong&gt;. You'll encounter a mix of ascents, descents, and flatter sections, characteristic of the Pyrenees foothills.

Q: Are there challenging routes for experienced touring cyclists?
A: Yes, Bordes offers several challenging routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15022580" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pont de Nay – Col du Soulor loop from Assat&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 92.7 km path with significant elevation gain, leading through mountainous landscapes and offering panoramic views. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e868923738/historic-loop-between-pau-tarbes-and-lourdes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic loop between Pau, Tarbes and Lourdes&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 68.8 km with substantial climbing.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or casual rides?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a more relaxed ride, there are 66 easy touring cycling routes available. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40950301" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baudreix Footbridge – View of the Nay Bridge loop from Baliros&lt;/a&gt;, a 21.4 km trail that winds through river valleys and can typically be completed in about 1 hour 18 minutes.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while cycling around Bordes?
A: The region boasts impressive natural features. You can explore dramatic landscapes like the &lt;strong&gt;Gorges d'Ehujarre&lt;/strong&gt; and majestic cirques such as the &lt;strong&gt;Cirque du Litor&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;Cirque de Sainte-Engrâce&lt;/strong&gt;. The area is also rich in rivers and waterfalls, contributing to a serene cycling environment. While cycling, you might pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2185442" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gave de Pau Cycle Bridge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural attractions accessible by touring bike near Bordes?
A: Yes, several cultural and historical sites are within cycling distance. The substantial town of &lt;strong&gt;Pau&lt;/strong&gt;, with its impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4119926" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;National Museum and Estate of the Château de Pau&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4119942" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palais Beaumont&lt;/a&gt;, is only about 10 km away. &lt;strong&gt;Lourdes&lt;/strong&gt;, a renowned pilgrimage center, is approximately 26 km away. The town of &lt;strong&gt;Oloron-Sainte-Marie&lt;/strong&gt;, with its historical churches, is also a charming destination.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the Bordes area?
A: Yes, many routes around Bordes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15022580" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pont de Nay – Col du Soulor loop from Assat&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38549060" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beaumont Park loop from Assat&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easier 19.6 km option.

Q: Are touring cycling routes around Bordes suitable for cycling with dogs?
A: While specific dog-friendly designations for cycling routes are not always available, the region's lush ecosystems and serene natural environment are generally welcoming for outdoor activities. Many valley routes and less technical paths could be suitable for cycling with a well-behaved dog, especially if they are accustomed to such outings. Always ensure your dog's safety and comfort, and check local regulations for specific areas.

Q: What is the best season for touring cycling in Bordes?
A: The Pyrenees region around Bordes offers good cycling conditions from spring through autumn. Spring brings blooming landscapes, while summer provides warm weather ideal for higher mountain passes. Autumn offers pleasant temperatures and beautiful fall foliage. Be aware that higher elevations can still have snow into late spring, and weather can change rapidly in the mountains.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Bordes?
A: The touring cycling routes in Bordes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an impressive average score of 4.37 stars from over 370 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from challenging mountain climbs to picturesque valley rides, and the blend of natural beauty with cultural landmarks. Over 4,000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore this varied region.

Q: Where can I find parking or access routes via public transport for touring cycling in Bordes?
A: Many routes around Bordes start from towns like Assat or Baliros, which typically offer local parking facilities. For public transport, major towns like Pau and Lourdes, which are connected to some cycling routes, have train stations and bus services that can facilitate access to the wider region. It's advisable to check local public transport schedules and bicycle carriage policies in advance.
